title: pilea johnstonii subsp. kiwuensis
intro: Pilea johnstonii subsp. kiwuensis, also known as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, is native to the tropical forests of Central Africa. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves which contain flavonoids and alkaloids with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pilea johnstonii subsp. kiwuensis include its ability to treat fever, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves, which can be dried, crushed, or infused in hot water to create teas or tonics for medicinal use.
